steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures on each side
steps.content: To the left of the blank: “Antonello da Messina and Alessandro Vittoria were among a group of Italian artists based in Venice in the sixteenth [century]” is an independent clause. To the right: “[This] group is referred to as the Venetian School” is an independent clause.

steps.title: Apply the rule for joining independent clauses
steps.content: Two independent clauses must be separated by a period, semicolon, or comma with a coordinating conjunction. Choice D uses a period to separate the two independent clauses and introduces the second sentence with “Fittingly,” (a transition word) properly followed by a comma. Options A and C create a comma splice by joining independent clauses with only a comma, while option B places the transition word at the end of the first sentence, deviating from standard structure. That matches choice D — D is correct.
